top_stories.recommended_action: Strait closure validates BE thesis completely - consider position sizing

24h_summary: MARKET IN CRISIS MODE: The Iran war has reached maximum escalation with Trump demanding 'unconditional surrender' and oil posting the largest weekly gain in futures history (+35%). The Strait of Hormuz is effectively closed with only 8 ships/day vs normal 120+, creating a supply shock. Meanwhile, February jobs crashed -92k vs +50k expected, unemployment rising to 4.4%, creating Fed policy confusion. The dual shock of energy crisis + economic weakness creates stagflation risk. Oil hitting $90+ validates our BE energy infrastructure thesis massively, but weak jobs data undermines our TLT short position as Fed governors signal dovish pivot. Defense stocks surging on Trump's 4x weapons production announcement. Market regime shifting to extreme risk-off with equity outflows accelerating.

key_themes.portfolio_implication: Massive validation of BE power infrastructure thesis
Threatens TLT short thesis via Fed dovish pivot
Classic stagflation setup - energy winners vs tech losers
Risk-off hurts growth stocks, validates defense/energy
